summary: Full regression test of iPad Pool feature, modell field, RBAC, contract encryption. Created comprehensive pytest suite. 25/26 tests passed, 1 skipped (cookie auth on /auth/me does not work - Bearer header required). Fixed missing libmagic1 system dependency that prevented backend from starting.

minor.issue: Response wrapper does not include 'modell' field even though it is updated correctly in DB. Verified via GET /ipads/{id}/history that DB stores it. Frontend may not see the new value in PUT response and would need a refetch.

Endpoint does not honor HttpOnly access_token cookie set by /api/auth/login. Login sets cookies (access_token + __cf_bm) but /api/auth/me requires Bearer header, returning 403 when only cookie is present. Logout endpoint works correctly. Spec says HttpOnly cookie auth should work end-to-end (withCredentials: true).
missing_fields label says 'Modell' but the check looks at ipad.typ (not ipad.modell). Inconsistent with new 'modell' column - label should match the actual field being validated.
libmagic1 system package was missing causing backend to crash on import magic. Fixed by sudo apt-get install -y libmagic1. Should be in Dockerfile / setup script.

action_items: Add 'modell' field to PUT /api/ipads/{id} response wrapper (server.py lines 2492-2504)
Make /api/auth/me read access_token from HttpOnly cookie when Authorization header is absent, otherwise HttpOnly cookie auth flow is broken on page reload
Update available-for-contracts missing_fields: either check ipad.modell (matching the new field) or rename label to 'Typ'
Add libmagic1 to backend system dependencies (Dockerfile/install script) to prevent startup failure
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 4214 lines - exceeds 700-line threshold significantly. Should be split into modules: auth.py, ipads.py, students.py, assignments.py, contracts.py, imports.py.
PUT /api/ipads/{ipad_id} response shape inconsistent: dict with 'message' + partial 'ipad' subset, while other PUT endpoints return the full updated model. Standardize.

Contract generation logic skips encryption when sus_geb is empty (contract_generator.py:122-128) - silent fallback to unencrypted ZIP for students without birthday. Should at minimum return missing_fields warning or refuse generation.

success_rate.backend: 96% (25/26 passed, 1 skipped due to cookie auth gap)
success_rate.frontend: Smoke-tested: login, dashboard render, admin nav (Schüler/iPads/Zuordnungen/Verträge/Einstellungen/Benutzer) all visible

seed_data_creation: Created test admin/user test data: TEST_reg_*, TEST_POOL_*, TEST_MU_*, TEST_CT_* etc. All cleaned up via autouse session fixture. Temporary user TEST_reg_* deleted via /admin/users/{id}/complete. One leftover from prior runs visible in UI: 'TEST_ContractWorkflow Student' (not created by this run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pool, modell field, RBAC, race conditions, Excel pool import, contract encryption all verified working. Key bugs to revisit after main agent fixes: (1) PUT /ipads response missing modell field, (2) /auth/me cookie auth gap, (3) available-for-contracts Modell-vs-typ mismatch. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_pool_regression.py is reusable as regression. Note: login is rate-limited at 5/minute - if running tests back-to-back, wait or use Bearer tokens.

test_credentials.note: Test users are created/deleted per test session using TEST_reg_<uuid> pattern

RBAC: user allowed: assignments delete/auto-assign, students delete/batch-delete, ipads delete, contracts batch-delete
Pool: Excel pool import with import_to_pool=true PASS
global ITNr uniqueness on second import PASS
GET /ipads/{id}/history returns owner_username + pool_history with by_username PASS
user delete preserves pool iPads with user_id=null PASS
Contracts: generate-contracts ZIP readable via zipfile PASS

rca_of_the_issue: 1) PUT /api/ipads/{ipad_id} response: server.py lines 2492-2504 build response dict manually but omit modell field. Update via DB works; only response shape is wrong. 2) /auth/me cookie auth: get_current_user dependency reads only Authorization header. Frontend uses withCredentials:true so cookie IS sent but ignored. Need to add cookie fallback in get_current_user. 3) Missing libmagic1: dependency not declared, only python-magic in requirements.txt. Install via apt-get.
